required_sections.detail: All required sections present and non-trivial. §3 Learning Objectives (6 measurable bullets), §4 Introduction (3 substantial paragraphs, >50w), §5 Core Concepts (9 subsections 5.1-5.9, far exceeds 100w), §10 Key Takeaways (6 bullets, >50w). No placeholder strings in required sections.

citation_density.detail: Primary resource [1] (realpython.com/python-iterators-iterables) cited ~13 times across §5.1-5.4 and Best Practices — well above the >=1 requirement. Supporting [2] (generators) cited in §5.5; supporting [3] (collections) cited across §5.6-5.9. In Core/Impl/Real-World, well over 60% of standalone factual assertions carry [N] markers. No fabricated citations: only [1],[2],[3] appear and all resolve to the 3 resources. (The token 'record[4]' on line 204 is array indexing, not a citation marker.)

sequence_drift.detail: No forward references. All primary concepts (iterable vs iterator, iterator protocol iter/next/StopIteration, for-loop under the hood, generator recap/yield/generator expression/lazy streaming, Counter+most_common, defaultdict int/list factory, namedtuple) are in 5.3 concepts_introduced. Prior refs valid: lists/comprehension (4.1), tuples/immutability/unpacking (4.2), sets/hashable/set comprehension (5.1), dict/get/setdefault/keys-values-items/KeyError (5.2), for loops/functions/generators (P1/P2). itertools mentioned only as 'beyond this topic's scope / going further' (further reading) — acceptable. No references to future topics (6.x OOP onward).

length_sanity.detail: Standard profile, ~2460 words reported. Within the 1500-6000 band acceptable for a corpus. Not wildly off.

verdict: PASS — all four checks clear; strict web_search citation density met, primary cited ~13x, no fabricated [N], no forward references (itertools flagged only as further reading), length in band.
